# @byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-faas/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-faas/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-faas/CHANGELOG.md)

> The following is **@byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas@2.x** document, **1.x** document see [fetch-v1.x](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/fetch-v1.x/packages/fetch-protocol-faas), [@byted-service/fetch@2.0.0 release log](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnMNUcH3YbXKEeZSX4YqCavc).

`@byted-service/fetch` faas protocol extention.

1. support `faas://faasid/xxx` [ByteFaaS](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn7wwvnEj9rahuyFhFhKiZAg).

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/fetch-protocol-faas --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Quick Start

> Currently it is built-in in @byted-service/fetch, actually can be use without any operation.

```ts
import { fetch } from '@byted-service/fetch';
import { protocol, handle } from '@byted-service/fetch-faas-consul';

fetch.register(protocol, handle);

(async () => {
    const resp = await fetch('faas://faasid/xxx');

    console.log(await resp.text());
})();
```

## API

### fetch(url[, options])

The parameters of `@byted-service/fetch` have been extended. Generally, It is set by framework , and user unaware.

<details>
  <summary>History</summary>

-   `v2.1.0`
    -   Mark `options.consulClient`, `options.consulClientOptions`, `options.faas.consulClient`, `options.faas.consulClientOptions` as deprecated.
    -   Support `options.consulOptions`, `options.consulLookupOptions`, `options.faas.consulOptions`, `options.faas.consulLookupOptions`.

</details>

-   `url`: request url.
-   `options`:
    -   `consul`: `Consul` instance, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
    -   `consulOptions`: `Consul` construction options, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
    -   `consulLookupOptions`: `consul.lookup()` options, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
    -   `faas`
        -   `consul`: `Consul` instance, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
        -   `consulOptions`: `Consul` construction options, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
        -   `consulLookupOptions`: `consul.lookup()` options, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
        -   `enableConsul`: Enable consul to lookup automatically faas servies(It is strongly recommended to enable online)，the default is true. Can be used in the situation that can not access to remote in the local developement environment.
        -   `silence`: Enable silence，the default is false. Can be used to close the warn log wher consul fails.
        -   `idc`: FaaS IDC，the default configuration(Generally is lf in development environment). It's priority is higher then region , can be used in local development environment to specify faas function.
        -   `region`: FaaS region，the default configuration(Local development has no), can be used in local development environment to specify faas function.

> [Consul related parameter priority](#consul-related-parameter-priority)

## Metrics

Related indicators and tags are the same as [@byted-service/fetch](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch/README.en.md#metrics), the modified part is listed below.

-   `to`: Server faasid.
-   `to_cluster`: Server cluster, can be set by `options.consul.cluster`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `to_idc`: Server idc, can be set by `options.consul.idc`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `ipv6`: The consul service found whether the returned ipv6 address, the default is `0`.
    -   `0`: No.
    -   `1`: Yes.

## Logger

Related tags are the same as [@byted-service/fetch](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch/README.en.md#logger), the modified part is listed below.

-   `to`: Server faasid.
-   `to_cluster`: Server cluster, can be set by `options.consul.cluster`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `to_idc`: Server idc, can be set by `options.consul.idc`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `ipv6`: The consul service found whether the returned ipv6 address, the default is `0`.
    -   `0`: No.
    -   `1`: Yes.
-   `use_faas_psm`: Is it using psm to request faas proxy.
    -   `0`: No.
    -   `1`: Yes.
-   `rip`: Real request ip.
-   `to_region`: Server region.

## FAQ

### Consul related parameter priority

-   `options.faas.consul` > `options.faas.consulOptions` > `options.consul` > `options.consulOptions`
-   `options.faas.consulLookupOptions` > `options.consulLookupOptions`

### Specify faas IDC in the local development environment

> e.g.: while developing, there is a faasid vqyvl29t in the boe , but local is boei18n.

If the consul service of the corresponding idc can be connected locally, it is recommended to select one parameter from the follow list to set Consul config options.

-   `options.consul`
-   `options.consulOptions`
-   `options.faas.consul`
-   `options.faas.consulOptions`

If unable to connect locally to corresponding consul service, see [Unable to connect locally to consul](#unable-to-connect-locally-to-consul).

### Unable to connect locally to consul

If you know the corresponding devbox，it is recommended to set [ROUTER_IP](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.md#%E5%A4%84%E7%90%86%E5%9B%A0route_ip%E6%88%96gateway%E4%B8%8Aconsul%E6%9C%8D%E5%8A%A1%E5%AF%BC%E8%87%B4%E7%9A%84%E8%AF%B7%E6%B1%82%E8%B6%85%E6%97%B6).

If can not be satisfied with setting ROUTE_IP, you can just give up consul,and initiate directly request with specific fass idc(**It is recommended online**).

```ts
await fetch('faas://vqyvl29t/xxx', {
    enableConsul: false,
    idc: 'boe', // your faas idc name
});
```

-   [faas idc config](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnIDqFcfRLvINChAn8jIvv0b#).

If the faas idc which you are using does not support ,see [Currently faas IDC does not support](#Currently-faas-IDC-does-not-support).

### Currently faas IDC does not support

You can choose one way to solve:

1. Initiate a merge request, the address is [https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/merge_requests](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/merge_requests), the file is [packages/fetch-protocol-faas/src/consts.ts](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/fetch-protocol-faas/src/consts.ts).

2. Initiate a issue , address is [https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/issues](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/issues).

3. Directly Use faas domain name to initiate request(In this time the request is the same as the general request).

```ts
await fetch('faas://vqyvl29t.fn-boe.bytedance.net/xxx');
```
